heading: Pictured Rocks National Lakeshore boundary revision
label: 460s–15 Pictured Rocks National Lakeshore boundary revision
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Omnibus Maritime and Coast Guard Improvements Act of 2002, and also as part of the Maritime Transportation Security Act of 2002, and not as part of which comprises this subchapter.Pub. L. 89–668

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of authorities, functions, personnel, and assets of the Coast Guard, including the authorities and functions of the Secretary of Transportation relating thereto, to the Department of Homeland Security, and for treatment of related references, see sections 468(b), 551(d), 552(d), and 557 of Title 6, Domestic Security, and the Department of Homeland Security Reorganization Plan of , as modified, set out as a note under .November 25, 2002section 542 of Title 6

children.heading: Transfer
children.label: a Transfer
children.text: As soon as practicable after , the Administrator of General Services may transfer to the Secretary, without consideration, administrative jurisdiction over, and management of, the public land.November 25, 2002

children.heading: Boundary revision
children.label: b Boundary revision
children.text: The boundary of the Lakeshore is revised to include the public land transferred under subsection (a).

children.heading: Availability of map
children.label: c Availability of map
children.text: The map shall be on file and available for public inspection in the appropriate offices of the National Park Service.
as part of the Lakeshore; and
in accordance with applicable laws (including regulations).

children.heading: Access to aids to navigation
children.label: e Access to aids to navigation
children.text: The Secretary of Transportation, in consultation with the Secretary, may access the front and rear range lights on the public land for the purposes of servicing, operating, maintaining, and repairing those lights.

children.heading: Lakeshore
children.label: 1 Lakeshore
children.text: The term “Lakeshore” means the Pictured Rocks National Lakeshore in the State of Michigan.

children.heading: Map
children.label: 2 Map
children.text: The term “map” means the map entitled “Proposed Addition to Pictured Rocks National Lakeshore”, numbered 625/80048, and dated April 2002.

children.heading: Public land
children.label: 3 Public land
children.text: The term “public land” means the approximately .32 acres of United States Coast Guard land and improvements to the land, including the United States Coast Guard Auxiliary Operations Station and the front and rear range lights, as depicted on the map.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 4 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: g Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary $225,000 to restore, preserve, and maintain the public land transferred under subsection (a).
